title: Earnings call transcript: BlackBerry Q4 2026 sees strong growth and stock uptick
text: BlackBerry delivered robust Q4 2026 performance with double-digit top-line growth and the eighth consecutive quarter of improving profitability. The company's stock surged 5.38% in premarket trading following the announcement. Q4 revenue reached $151.2 million with 10% year-over-year growth. Earnings per share were $0.06, exceeding the $0.045 forecast. Gross margins improved to 78.2% in Q4. Adjusted EBITDA reached $36.1 million for Q4. The QNX Division achieved record revenue of $78.7 million with 20% year-over-year growth. The royalty backlog reached approximately $950 million, indicating strong future revenue visibility. Secure Communications revenue reached $72.5 million, exceeding guidance by 12% with 8% year-over-year growth, driven by digital sovereignty demand and military contracts. Management emphasized expansion into non-automotive sectors including robotics, industrial automation, and medical instrumentation. The company is developing the Alloy Core platform to move deeper into automotive software stacks. CEO John Giamatteo stated the turnaround is complete, positioning BlackBerry as a growth story moving forward.
